results.question: Compare Verstappen vs Russell mini-sector dominance at Spa 2025 qualifying — was Verstappen's advantage concentrated in S2 or distributed?
results.author_note: The Race / Edd Straw Spa qualifying analyses framing S2 as the dominant sector.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Verstappen's 0.351s overall advantage over Russell at Spa 2025 qualifying was heavily concentrated — and then some — in Sector 2, where he was 0.678s faster (42.785s vs 43.463s), accounting for 193% of the total gap. Russell actually beat Verstappen in S1 by 0.294s (29.761s vs 30.055s) and marginally in S3 by 0.069s (27.857s vs 27.926s), partially cancelling out Verstappen's dominant S2 advantage. In short, Verstappen's edge was entirely a Sector 2 phenomenon — the long, high-speed middle sector of Spa — while Russell held the upper hand in the other two sectors.
results.answerReasoning: The query returns best-sector times for each driver and calculates the gap per sector as a percentage of the overall lap gap. Verstappen's S2 gap of +0.678s exceeds the total lap gap of 0.351s (193.2% of gap), while Russell's S1 (-0.294s, -83.8%) and S3 (-0.069s, -19.7%) advantages partially offset it. This clearly shows the advantage is concentrated in S2, not distributed.
results.generationNotes: The query compares Verstappen (driver 1) and Russell (driver 63) at session 9935 (Spa 2025 qualifying). It pulls all valid laps with sector times from core.laps_enriched, computes each driver's personal best lap time and best individual sector times (S1, S2, S3), then pivots to a single comparison row. The key output is the raw gap per sector (Russell minus Verstappen, positive = Verstappen faster) and the percentage of the total lap gap attributable to each sector — this directly answers whether Verstappen's advantage was concentrated in S2 (the long Raidillon/Pouhon section) or distributed across all three sectors. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9935)
results.sql:
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        le.driver_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy,
        le.compound_name,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    WHERE le.session_key = 9935
      AND le.driver_number IN (1, 63)
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
),
best_laps AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        lap_number,
        lap_duration,
        duration_sector_1,
        duration_sector_2,
        duration_sector_3,
        compound_name,
        tyre_age_on_lap,
        is_valid,
        is_personal_best_proxy,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number ORDER BY lap_duration ASC) AS rn_overall,
        MIN(duration_sector_1) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number) AS best_s1,
        MIN(duration_sector_2) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number) AS best_s2,
        MIN(duration_sector_3) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number) AS best_s3
    FROM base
    WHERE is_valid = TRUE
      AND duration_sector_1 IS NOT NULL
      AND duration_sector_2 IS NOT NULL
      AND duration_sector_3 IS NOT NULL
),
driver_bests AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        MIN(lap_duration)       AS best_lap,
        MIN(duration_sector_1)  AS best_s1,
        MIN(duration_sector_2)  AS best_s2,
        MIN(duration_sector_3)  AS best_s3,
        COUNT(*)                AS valid_lap_count
    FROM best_laps
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name
),
pivoted AS (
    SELECT
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 1  THEN best_lap  END) AS vst_best_lap,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 63 THEN best_lap  END) AS rus_best_lap,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 1  THEN best_s1   END) AS vst_s1,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 63 THEN best_s1   END) AS rus_s1,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 1  THEN best_s2   END) AS vst_s2,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 63 THEN best_s2   END) AS rus_s2,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 1  THEN best_s3   END) AS vst_s3,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 63 THEN best_s3   END) AS rus_s3,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 1  THEN valid_lap_count END) AS vst_valid_laps,
        MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 63 THEN valid_lap_count END) AS rus_valid_laps
    FROM driver_bests
)
SELECT
    ROUND(vst_best_lap::numeric, 3)                                     AS verstappen_best_lap,
    ROUND(rus_best_lap::numeric, 3)                                     AS russell_best_lap,
    ROUND((rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap)::numeric, 3)                    AS gap_overall_sec,

    ROUND(vst_s1::numeric, 3)                                           AS verstappen_best_s1,
    ROUND(rus_s1::numeric, 3)                                           AS russell_best_s1,
    ROUND((rus_s1 - vst_s1)::numeric, 3)                                AS gap_s1_sec,

    ROUND(vst_s2::numeric, 3)                                           AS verstappen_best_s2,
    ROUND(rus_s2::numeric, 3)                                           AS russell_best_s2,
    ROUND((rus_s2 - vst_s2)::numeric, 3)                                AS gap_s2_sec,

    ROUND(vst_s3::numeric, 3)                                           AS verstappen_best_s3,
    ROUND(rus_s3::numeric, 3)                                           AS russell_best_s3,
    ROUND((rus_s3 - vst_s3)::numeric, 3)                                AS gap_s3_sec,

    -- Share of total gap attributable to each sector
    CASE WHEN (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) <> 0
         THEN ROUND(((rus_s1 - vst_s1) / (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) * 100)::numeric, 1)
         ELSE NULL END                                                  AS s1_pct_of_gap,
    CASE WHEN (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) <> 0
         THEN ROUND(((rus_s2 - vst_s2) / (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) * 100)::numeric, 1)
         ELSE NULL END                                                  AS s2_pct_of_gap,
    CASE WHEN (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) <> 0
         THEN ROUND(((rus_s3 - vst_s3) / (rus_best_lap - vst_best_lap) * 100)::numeric, 1)
         ELSE NULL END                                                  AS s3_pct_of_gap,

    vst_valid_laps                                                      AS verstappen_valid_laps,
    rus_valid_laps                                                      AS russell_valid_laps
FROM pivoted

results.question: Where was Hamilton losing time to Leclerc on his fastest Q3 lap at Imola 2025 — full sector splits?
results.author_note: Motorsport.com Imola intra-team Ferrari telemetry comparison (Hamilton vs Leclerc).
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: At Imola 2025 Q3, Hamilton (78.265s) was actually faster than Leclerc (79.048s) across all three sectors — Hamilton did not lose time to Leclerc anywhere. Hamilton was quicker by 0.479s in Sector 1 (24.534 vs 25.013), 0.143s in Sector 2 (27.480 vs 27.623), and 0.161s in Sector 3 (26.251 vs 26.412), for a total advantage of 0.783s.
results.answerReasoning: The gap values are all negative (Hamilton minus Leclerc), meaning Hamilton was faster in every sector. The question assumes Hamilton was losing time to Leclerc, but the data shows the opposite — Hamilton was quicker in S1, S2, and S3 on his best Q3 lap.
results.generationNotes: The question asks for a sector-by-sector comparison of Hamilton (driver 44) vs Leclerc (driver 16) on their fastest valid Q3 laps at Imola 2025 (session_key 9987). The runtime context resolves session_key=9987 and flags driver_number=16 (Leclerc), but Hamilton (44) must be included for the comparison. Using core.laps_enriched filtered to session_key=9987 and both drivers, I pick each driver's single fastest valid lap via DISTINCT ON, then pivot into a single row showing each driver's sector splits and the delta (Hamilton minus Leclerc) per sector and overall — positive delta means Hamilton is slower in that sector. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9987)
results.sql:
WITH session_fastest AS (
  SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    lap_number,
    lap_duration,
    duration_sector_1,
    duration_sector_2,
    duration_sector_3
  FROM core.laps_enriched
  WHERE session_key = 9987
    AND driver_number IN (44, 16)
    AND is_valid = true
    AND lap_duration IS NOT NULL
),
best_laps AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (driver_number)
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    lap_number,
    lap_duration,
    duration_sector_1,
    duration_sector_2,
    duration_sector_3
  FROM session_fastest
  ORDER BY driver_number, lap_duration ASC
),
pivoted AS (
  SELECT
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 44 THEN driver_name END) AS ham_name,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN driver_name END) AS lec_name,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 44 THEN lap_duration END)       AS ham_lap,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN lap_duration END)       AS lec_lap,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 44 THEN duration_sector_1 END)  AS ham_s1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_1 END)  AS lec_s1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 44 THEN duration_sector_2 END)  AS ham_s2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_2 END)  AS lec_s2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 44 THEN duration_sector_3 END)  AS ham_s3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_3 END)  AS lec_s3
  FROM best_laps
)
SELECT
  ham_name                                   AS hamilton_name,
  lec_name                                   AS leclerc_name,
  ROUND(ham_lap::numeric, 3)                 AS ham_best_lap,
  ROUND(lec_lap::numeric, 3)                 AS lec_best_lap,
  ROUND((ham_lap - lec_lap)::numeric, 3)     AS gap_total,
  ROUND(ham_s1::numeric, 3)                  AS ham_s1,
  ROUND(lec_s1::numeric, 3)                  AS lec_s1,
  ROUND((ham_s1 - lec_s1)::numeric, 3)       AS gap_s1,
  ROUND(ham_s2::numeric, 3)                  AS ham_s2,
  ROUND(lec_s2::numeric, 3)                  AS lec_s2,
  ROUND((ham_s2 - lec_s2)::numeric, 3)       AS gap_s2,
  ROUND(ham_s3::numeric, 3)                  AS ham_s3,
  ROUND(lec_s3::numeric, 3)                  AS lec_s3,
  ROUND((ham_s3 - lec_s3)::numeric, 3)       AS gap_s3
FROM pivoted

results.question: At Suzuka 2025, did Norris's qualifying advantage over Piastri come from S1 (high-speed esses) or S3 (final chicane)?
results.author_note: Autosport Suzuka qualifying analyses splitting the S-curves vs the final chicane debate.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Norris's qualifying advantage over Piastri at Suzuka 2025 came primarily from S1 (the high-speed Esses). Norris was 0.156s faster than Piastri in S1 (30.358s vs 30.514s), which more than offset Piastri's advantages in S2 (+0.104s for Piastri) and S3 (+0.102s for Piastri). The net result was Norris edging Piastri by 0.032s on best lap time (86.995s vs 87.027s).
results.answerReasoning: The s1_delta_nor_minus_pia of -0.156 shows Norris was clearly faster in S1, while s2 and s3 deltas are positive (meaning Piastri was faster there). The absolute S1 gap (0.156s) is larger than both the S2 gap (0.104s) and S3 gap (0.102s), confirming S1 as the sector where Norris built his overall lap advantage.
results.generationNotes: To determine where Norris's qualifying advantage (or deficit) over Piastri came from, I compare their personal best sector times across S1, S2, and S3 for session_key 10002. Driver 4 = Norris, Driver 81 = Piastri. Negative deltas (Norris minus Piastri) mean Norris was faster in that sector. The query computes best valid sector times for each driver, calculates the per-sector delta, and identifies which sector shows the largest absolute gap — answering whether the advantage came from S1 (high-speed esses) or S3 (final chicane). | session_pin_verified(session_key=10002)
results.sql:
WITH sector_bests AS (
  SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    MIN(duration_sector_1) FILTER (WHERE is_valid AND duration_sector_1 > 0) AS best_s1,
    MIN(duration_sector_2) FILTER (WHERE is_valid AND duration_sector_2 > 0) AS best_s2,
    MIN(duration_sector_3) FILTER (WHERE is_valid AND duration_sector_3 > 0) AS best_s3,
    MIN(lap_duration)      FILTER (WHERE is_valid AND lap_duration > 0)      AS best_lap
  FROM core.laps_enriched
  WHERE session_key = 10002
    AND driver_number IN (4, 81)
  GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name
),
norris AS (
  SELECT * FROM sector_bests WHERE driver_number = 4
),
piastri AS (
  SELECT * FROM sector_bests WHERE driver_number = 81
)
SELECT
  n.driver_name                                    AS norris_name,
  p.driver_name                                    AS piastri_name,
  ROUND((n.best_lap  - p.best_lap )::numeric, 3)   AS lap_delta_nor_minus_pia,
  ROUND((n.best_s1   - p.best_s1  )::numeric, 3)   AS s1_delta_nor_minus_pia,
  ROUND((n.best_s2   - p.best_s2  )::numeric, 3)   AS s2_delta_nor_minus_pia,
  ROUND((n.best_s3   - p.best_s3  )::numeric, 3)   AS s3_delta_nor_minus_pia,
  n.best_s1  AS norris_best_s1,
  p.best_s1  AS piastri_best_s1,
  n.best_s2  AS norris_best_s2,
  p.best_s2  AS piastri_best_s2,
  n.best_s3  AS norris_best_s3,
  p.best_s3  AS piastri_best_s3,
  n.best_lap AS norris_best_lap,
  p.best_lap AS piastri_best_lap,
  CASE
    WHEN ABS(n.best_s1 - p.best_s1) >= ABS(n.best_s3 - p.best_s3)
     AND ABS(n.best_s1 - p.best_s1) >= ABS(n.best_s2 - p.best_s2)
    THEN 'S1 (High-Speed Esses)'
    WHEN ABS(n.best_s3 - p.best_s3) >= ABS(n.best_s1 - p.best_s1)
     AND ABS(n.best_s3 - p.best_s3) >= ABS(n.best_s2 - p.best_s2)
    THEN 'S3 (Final Chicane)'
    ELSE 'S2 (Middle Sector)'
  END AS largest_sector_gap
FROM norris n
CROSS JOIN piastri p

results.question: Which corners did Verstappen gain on Norris through Sector 2 at Silverstone 2025 — Maggotts, Becketts, or Chapel?
results.author_note: Sky F1 SkyPad Maggotts-Becketts complex breakdowns at Silverstone.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: The data does not break Sector 2 down into individual corners (Maggotts, Becketts, Chapel) — it only provides a single combined Sector 2 time. What the data does show is that Norris was faster through Sector 2 (the Maggotts/Becketts/Chapel complex as a whole) on most laps, including laps 46–48 and 52, where Norris held advantages of 0.264–0.671 seconds. Verstappen was faster in Sector 2 on laps 49 (by 0.250s), 50 (by 0.004s), and 51 (by 0.079s). Without mini-sector or telemetry data pinpointing individual corners, it is impossible to say whether Verstappen's gains came specifically at Maggotts, Becketts, or Chapel.
results.answerReasoning: The SQL query aggregates Sector 2 as a single duration. The rows confirm Norris dominated S2 overall, with Verstappen only edging S2 on laps 49–51 by small margins. No corner-level breakdown exists in the provided data to attribute gains to specific corners within the complex.
results.generationNotes: The question asks about Sector 2 at Silverstone (session_key 9947), which covers the Maggotts, Becketts, and Chapel complex. Since telemetry mini-sector data is not available in the schema (only full sector splits), I compare Verstappen (driver 1) vs Norris (driver 4) on a lap-by-lap basis using duration_sector_2 from core.laps_enriched. A negative delta_s2 means Verstappen was faster through that sector complex. The query joins both drivers' valid laps on lap_number and computes per-sector deltas, labelling S2 outcomes with the corner names for interpretability. This gives the analyst the full picture of which laps Verstappen gained or lost through Maggotts/Becketts/Chapel relative to Norris. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9947)
results.sql:
WITH ver_laps AS (
    SELECT
        lap_number,
        lap_duration,
        duration_sector_1,
        duration_sector_2,
        duration_sector_3,
        is_valid,
        compound_name,
        tyre_age_on_lap
    FROM core.laps_enriched
    WHERE session_key = 9947
      AND driver_number = 1
      AND is_valid = TRUE
),
nor_laps AS (
    SELECT
        lap_number,
        lap_duration,
        duration_sector_1,
        duration_sector_2,
        duration_sector_3,
        is_valid,
        compound_name,
        tyre_age_on_lap
    FROM core.laps_enriched
    WHERE session_key = 9947
      AND driver_number = 4
      AND is_valid = TRUE
),
comparison AS (
    SELECT
        v.lap_number,
        v.compound_name AS ver_compound,
        n.compound_name AS nor_compound,
        v.tyre_age_on_lap AS ver_tyre_age,
        n.tyre_age_on_lap AS nor_tyre_age,
        ROUND(v.duration_sector_1::numeric, 3) AS ver_s1,
        ROUND(n.duration_sector_1::numeric, 3) AS nor_s1,
        ROUND((v.duration_sector_1 - n.duration_sector_1)::numeric, 3) AS delta_s1,
        ROUND(v.duration_sector_2::numeric, 3) AS ver_s2,
        ROUND(n.duration_sector_2::numeric, 3) AS nor_s2,
        ROUND((v.duration_sector_2 - n.duration_sector_2)::numeric, 3) AS delta_s2,
        ROUND(v.duration_sector_3::numeric, 3) AS ver_s3,
        ROUND(n.duration_sector_3::numeric, 3) AS nor_s3,
        ROUND((v.duration_sector_3 - n.duration_sector_3)::numeric, 3) AS delta_s3,
        ROUND(v.lap_duration::numeric, 3) AS ver_lap,
        ROUND(n.lap_duration::numeric, 3) AS nor_lap,
        ROUND((v.lap_duration - n.lap_duration)::numeric, 3) AS delta_lap
    FROM ver_laps v
    JOIN nor_laps n ON v.lap_number = n.lap_number
)
SELECT
    lap_number,
    ver_compound,
    nor_compound,
    ver_tyre_age,
    nor_tyre_age,
    ver_s1, nor_s1,
    delta_s1,
    CASE WHEN delta_s1 < 0 THEN 'VER faster' WHEN delta_s1 > 0 THEN 'NOR faster' ELSE 'Equal' END AS s1_winner,
    ver_s2, nor_s2,
    delta_s2,
    CASE WHEN delta_s2 < 0 THEN 'VER faster (Maggotts/Becketts/Chapel)' WHEN delta_s2 > 0 THEN 'NOR faster (Maggotts/Becketts/Chapel)' ELSE 'Equal' END AS s2_winner,
    ver_s3, nor_s3,
    delta_s3,
    CASE WHEN delta_s3 < 0 THEN 'VER faster' WHEN delta_s3 > 0 THEN 'NOR faster' ELSE 'Equal' END AS s3_winner,
    ver_lap, nor_lap, delta_lap
FROM comparison
ORDER BY lap_number
LIMIT 100

results.question: Compare Russell's Q2 vs Q3 mini-sector dominance at Las Vegas 2025 — did he sustain his S1 advantage into Q3?
results.author_note: RaceFans qualifying session-over-session pace comparison format (Vegas).
results.adequacyReason: The query returned no rows, so the question remains unanswered.
results.answer: No rows matched this question with the current context.
results.generationNotes: The question asks whether Russell (63) sustained his S1 advantage from Q2 into Q3 at Las Vegas 2025 (session_key=9858). I compare Russell vs Gasly/Piastri (driver 10 from resolved entities) across Q2 and Q3 sessions. Using core.laps_enriched filtered to session_key=9858 and drivers 63 and 10, I compute best and average sector times per driver per qualifying session, then pivot to show side-by-side deltas (Russell minus opponent) for S1, S2, S3, and overall lap. A derived 's1_dominance' label makes it easy to see whether Russell's S1 edge held in Q3. Results are ordered Q2 then Q3 for direct comparison. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9858)
results.sql:
WITH q_laps AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        le.driver_name,
        le.session_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy,
        le.delta_to_fastest,
        le.pct_from_fastest
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    WHERE le.session_key = 9858
      AND le.driver_number IN (63, 10)
      AND le.session_name IN ('Q2', 'Q3')
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_1 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_2 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_3 IS NOT NULL
),
best_per_session AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        session_name,
        MIN(lap_duration)                                          AS best_lap,
        MIN(duration_sector_1)                                     AS best_s1,
        MIN(duration_sector_2)                                     AS best_s2,
        MIN(duration_sector_3)                                     AS best_s3,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_valid)                           AS valid_laps,
        AVG(duration_sector_1) FILTER (WHERE is_valid)             AS avg_s1,
        AVG(duration_sector_2) FILTER (WHERE is_valid)             AS avg_s2,
        AVG(duration_sector_3) FILTER (WHERE is_valid)             AS avg_s3
    FROM q_laps
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, session_name
),
pivoted AS (
    SELECT
        b63.session_name,
        b63.best_lap                                               AS rus_best_lap,
        b10.best_lap                                               AS pie_best_lap,
        ROUND((b63.best_lap - b10.best_lap)::numeric, 3)           AS lap_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        b63.best_s1                                                AS rus_best_s1,
        b10.best_s1                                                AS pie_best_s1,
        ROUND((b63.best_s1 - b10.best_s1)::numeric, 3)            AS s1_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        b63.best_s2                                                AS rus_best_s2,
        b10.best_s2                                                AS pie_best_s2,
        ROUND((b63.best_s2 - b10.best_s2)::numeric, 3)            AS s2_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        b63.best_s3                                                AS rus_best_s3,
        b10.best_s3                                                AS pie_best_s3,
        ROUND((b63.best_s3 - b10.best_s3)::numeric, 3)            AS s3_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        ROUND(b63.avg_s1::numeric, 3)                              AS rus_avg_s1,
        ROUND(b10.avg_s1::numeric, 3)                              AS pie_avg_s1,
        ROUND((b63.avg_s1 - b10.avg_s1)::numeric, 3)              AS avg_s1_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        ROUND(b63.avg_s2::numeric, 3)                              AS rus_avg_s2,
        ROUND(b10.avg_s2::numeric, 3)                              AS pie_avg_s2,
        ROUND((b63.avg_s2 - b10.avg_s2)::numeric, 3)              AS avg_s2_delta_rus_minus_pie,
        ROUND(b63.avg_s3::numeric, 3)                              AS rus_avg_s3,
        ROUND(b10.avg_s3::numeric, 3)                              AS pie_avg_s3,
        ROUND((b63.avg_s3 - b10.avg_s3)::numeric, 3)              AS avg_s3_delta_rus_minus_pie
    FROM best_per_session b63
    JOIN best_per_session b10
      ON b63.session_name = b10.session_name
     AND b63.driver_number = 63
     AND b10.driver_number = 10
)
SELECT
    session_name,
    rus_best_lap,
    pie_best_lap,
    lap_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    rus_best_s1,
    pie_best_s1,
    s1_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    CASE
        WHEN s1_delta_rus_minus_pie < 0 THEN 'RUS leads S1'
        WHEN s1_delta_rus_minus_pie > 0 THEN 'PIE leads S1'
        ELSE 'Tied S1'
    END                                                            AS s1_dominance,
    rus_best_s2,
    pie_best_s2,
    s2_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    rus_best_s3,
    pie_best_s3,
    s3_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    rus_avg_s1,
    pie_avg_s1,
    avg_s1_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    rus_avg_s2,
    pie_avg_s2,
    avg_s2_delta_rus_minus_pie,
    rus_avg_s3,
    pie_avg_s3,
    avg_s3_delta_rus_minus_pie
FROM pivoted
ORDER BY
    CASE session_name WHEN 'Q2' THEN 1 WHEN 'Q3' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

On a theoretical best-sector basis, Piastri was actually 0.155s faster overall, meaning Leclerc's pole lap of 79.007s beat Piastri's 78.982s through a superior combined lap rather than sector-by-sector dominance. The query does not include mini-sector (segment) breakdowns or Q2 vs Q3 session splits, so it is not possible to identify which mini-sector flipped between Q2 and Q3 from the available data.

The advantage_characterisation field explicitly states 'Mixed — advantage varies by sector (not single-corner)'. results.generationNotes: The question asks whether Leclerc's pole advantage over Piastri (drivers 16 and 81) at Monza 2025 qualifying (session_key=9908) was sustained across all sectors or concentrated in one area, and which mini-sector flipped between Q2 and Q3. Since mini-sector data is not available in the schema (no mini-sector columns exist), I focus on the three macro sectors available in core.laps_enriched. The query computes: (1) best valid lap times for both drivers, (2) best individual sector times (S1, S2, S3) for each driver, (3) the gap in each sector (LEC minus PIA, negative = LEC faster), (4) theoretical best lap from summing best sectors, and (5) a characterisation of whether the advantage is sustained or sector-specific. This covers the full-sector dimension of the question. The lap-by-lap CTE is included for completeness but the main output is the aggregated sector comparison. Note: mini-sector granularity is not available in the warehouse schema, so the answer is limited to the three macro sectors. | The only fix needed was replacing `driver_name` with `full_name AS driver_name` in the `session_drivers_cte` CTE, since `core.session_drivers` has `full_name` rather than `driver_name`. All downstream CTEs reference `sd.driver_name` which is now correctly aliased from `full_name`. The rest of the query logic (sector comparisons, gap calculations, advantage characterisation) remains unchanged. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_verified(session_key=9908)

previewRows.advantage_characterisation: Mixed — advantage varies by sector (not single-corner)

results.sql:
WITH session_drivers_cte AS (
    SELECT driver_number, full_name AS driver_name, team_name
    FROM core.session_drivers
    WHERE session_key = 9908
      AND driver_number IN (16, 81)
),
best_laps AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        sd.driver_name,
        sd.team_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy,
        le.compound_name,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap,
        le.position_end_of_lap,
        le.track_flag,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY le.driver_number
            ORDER BY CASE WHEN le.is_valid THEN le.lap_duration ELSE NULL END ASC NULLS LAST
        ) AS rn_valid
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    JOIN session_drivers_cte sd
        ON le.driver_number = sd.driver_number
    WHERE le.session_key = 9908
      AND le.is_valid = TRUE
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_1 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_2 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_3 IS NOT NULL
),
all_laps_ranked AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        sd.driver_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    JOIN session_drivers_cte sd
        ON le.driver_number = sd.driver_number
    WHERE le.session_key = 9908
      AND le.is_valid = TRUE
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
),
final AS (
    SELECT
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN lap_duration END) AS lec_best_lap,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN lap_duration END) AS pia_best_lap,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN lap_duration END)
            - MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN lap_duration END) AS gap_overall,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_1 END) AS lec_best_s1,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_1 END) AS pia_best_s1,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_1 END)
            - MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_1 END) AS gap_s1,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_2 END) AS lec_best_s2,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_2 END) AS pia_best_s2,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_2 END)
            - MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_2 END) AS gap_s2,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_3 END) AS lec_best_s3,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_3 END) AS pia_best_s3,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_3 END)
            - MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_3 END) AS gap_s3,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_1 END)
            + MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_2 END)
            + MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 16 THEN duration_sector_3 END) AS lec_theoretical_best,
        MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_1 END)
            + MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_2 END)
            + MIN(CASE WHEN driver_number = 81 THEN duration_sector_3 END) AS pia_theoretical_best
    FROM all_laps_ranked
)
SELECT
    f.lec_best_lap,
    f.pia_best_lap,
    ROUND((f.gap_overall)::numeric, 4)                          AS gap_overall_lec_minus_pia,
    f.lec_best_s1,
    f.pia_best_s1,
    ROUND((f.gap_s1)::numeric, 4)                               AS gap_s1_lec_minus_pia,
    CASE WHEN f.gap_s1 < 0 THEN 'LEC' WHEN f.gap_s1 > 0 THEN 'PIA' ELSE 'TIED' END AS s1_faster,
    f.lec_best_s2,
    f.pia_best_s2,
    ROUND((f.gap_s2)::numeric, 4)                               AS gap_s2_lec_minus_pia,
    CASE WHEN f.gap_s2 < 0 THEN 'LEC' WHEN f.gap_s2 > 0 THEN 'PIA' ELSE 'TIED' END AS s2_faster,
    f.lec_best_s3,
    f.pia_best_s3,
    ROUND((f.gap_s3)::numeric, 4)                               AS gap_s3_lec_minus_pia,
    CASE WHEN f.gap_s3 < 0 THEN 'LEC' WHEN f.gap_s3 > 0 THEN 'PIA' ELSE 'TIED' END AS s3_faster,
    f.lec_theoretical_best,
    f.pia_theoretical_best,
    ROUND((f.lec_theoretical_best - f.pia_theoretical_best)::numeric, 4) AS theoretical_gap_lec_minus_pia,
    CASE
        WHEN (f.gap_s1 < 0 AND f.gap_s2 < 0 AND f.gap_s3 < 0) THEN 'LEC advantage sustained across ALL sectors'
        WHEN (f.gap_s1 > 0 AND f.gap_s2 > 0 AND f.gap_s3 > 0) THEN 'PIA faster in ALL sectors'
        ELSE 'Mixed — advantage varies by sector (not single-corner)'
    END AS advantage_characterisation
FROM final f
LIMIT 1

results.question: At Hungary 2025, where did Norris lose time to Russell across S1, S2, and S3 simultaneously, and was the deficit consistent or corner-specific (T4 vs T11 vs T14)?
results.author_note: Motorsport.com Hungaroring deep-dives triangulating sector loss against named corners (T4 / T11 / T14).
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Across 256 comparable valid laps at Hungary 2025, Norris was slower than Russell in all three sectors simultaneously on 72 laps, but the deficit was not consistent — it was heavily corner-specific. S1 (T4 area) was Norris's biggest structural weakness: he was slower there on 168 of 256 laps with an average deficit of +0.061s. S2 (T11 area) and S3 (T14 area) actually favoured Norris on average (-0.064s and -0.029s respectively), though he was still slower in S2 on 108 laps and S3 on 128 laps. Overall, Norris was marginally faster on average (-0.032s per lap), meaning the time loss to Russell was concentrated in S1/T4, while Norris recovered and often gained in S2 and S3.
results.answerReasoning: The aggregated row shows avg_s1_delta = +0.061 (Norris slower), avg_s2_delta = -0.064 (Norris faster), avg_s3_delta = -0.029 (Norris faster), with laps_norris_slower_s1=168, laps_norris_slower_s2=108, laps_norris_slower_s3=128 out of 256 laps. Only 72 laps saw Norris slower in all three sectors simultaneously. The avg_lap_delta of -0.032 confirms Norris was net faster overall, so the S1 deficit was the primary area of loss to Russell.
results.generationNotes: The question asks where Norris (driver 4) lost time to Russell (driver 63) across S1, S2, and S3 at Hungary 2025 (session_key=9928), and whether the deficit was consistent or corner-specific (T4=S1, T11=S2, T14=S3 at Hungaroring). I join valid laps from core.laps_enriched for both drivers on matching lap numbers, compute per-lap sector deltas (Norris minus Russell, positive = Norris slower), classify each lap by which sectors Norris was slower in, and cross-join aggregate statistics (avg/stddev/min/max per sector delta, counts of laps where Norris was slower in each sector) to show both the lap-by-lap pattern and the overall summary in one result set. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9928)
results.sql:
WITH sector_laps AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        sd.broadcast_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.compound_name,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap,
        le.is_valid,
        le.position_end_of_lap,
        le.track_flag
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    JOIN core.session_drivers sd
        ON le.session_key = sd.session_key
        AND le.driver_number = sd.driver_number
    WHERE le.session_key = 9928
      AND le.driver_number IN (4, 63)
      AND le.is_valid = TRUE
      AND le.duration_sector_1 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_2 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_3 IS NOT NULL
),
norris AS (
    SELECT * FROM sector_laps WHERE driver_number = 4
),
russell AS (
    SELECT * FROM sector_laps WHERE driver_number = 63
),
matched AS (
    SELECT
        n.lap_number,
        n.broadcast_name AS norris_name,
        r.broadcast_name AS russell_name,
        n.compound_name AS norris_compound,
        r.compound_name AS russell_compound,
        n.tyre_age_on_lap AS norris_tyre_age,
        r.tyre_age_on_lap AS russell_tyre_age,
        n.lap_duration AS norris_lap,
        r.lap_duration AS russell_lap,
        ROUND((n.lap_duration - r.lap_duration)::numeric, 3) AS lap_delta_nor_minus_rus,
        n.duration_sector_1 AS norris_s1,
        r.duration_sector_1 AS russell_s1,
        ROUND((n.duration_sector_1 - r.duration_sector_1)::numeric, 3) AS s1_delta,
        n.duration_sector_2 AS norris_s2,
        r.duration_sector_2 AS russell_s2,
        ROUND((n.duration_sector_2 - r.duration_sector_2)::numeric, 3) AS s2_delta,
        n.duration_sector_3 AS norris_s3,
        r.duration_sector_3 AS russell_s3,
        ROUND((n.duration_sector_3 - r.duration_sector_3)::numeric, 3) AS s3_delta,
        n.position_end_of_lap AS norris_pos,
        r.position_end_of_lap AS russell_pos,
        n.track_flag
    FROM norris n
    JOIN russell r ON n.lap_number = r.lap_number
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT
        norris_name,
        russell_name,
        COUNT(*) AS laps_compared,
        ROUND(AVG(lap_delta_nor_minus_rus)::numeric, 3) AS avg_lap_delta,
        ROUND(AVG(s1_delta)::numeric, 3) AS avg_s1_delta,
        ROUND(AVG(s2_delta)::numeric, 3) AS avg_s2_delta,
        ROUND(AVG(s3_delta)::numeric, 3) AS avg_s3_delta,
        ROUND(STDDEV(s1_delta)::numeric, 3) AS stddev_s1_delta,
        ROUND(STDDEV(s2_delta)::numeric, 3) AS stddev_s2_delta,
        ROUND(STDDEV(s3_delta)::numeric, 3) AS stddev_s3_delta,
        ROUND(MIN(s1_delta)::numeric, 3) AS min_s1_delta,
        ROUND(MAX(s1_delta)::numeric, 3) AS max_s1_delta,
        ROUND(MIN(s2_delta)::numeric, 3) AS min_s2_delta,
        ROUND(MAX(s2_delta)::numeric, 3) AS max_s2_delta,
        ROUND(MIN(s3_delta)::numeric, 3) AS min_s3_delta,
        ROUND(MAX(s3_delta)::numeric, 3) AS max_s3_delta,
        SUM(CASE WHEN s1_delta > 0 AND s2_delta > 0 AND s3_delta > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS laps_norris_slower_all_sectors,
        SUM(CASE WHEN s1_delta > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS laps_norris_slower_s1,
        SUM(CASE WHEN s2_delta > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS laps_norris_slower_s2,
        SUM(CASE WHEN s3_delta > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS laps_norris_slower_s3
    FROM matched
    GROUP BY norris_name, russell_name
)
SELECT
    m.lap_number,
    m.norris_compound,
    m.norris_tyre_age,
    m.russell_compound,
    m.russell_tyre_age,
    m.norris_lap,
    m.russell_lap,
    m.lap_delta_nor_minus_rus,
    m.norris_s1,
    m.russell_s1,
    m.s1_delta,
    m.norris_s2,
    m.russell_s2,
    m.s2_delta,
    m.norris_s3,
    m.russell_s3,
    m.s3_delta,
    CASE
        WHEN m.s1_delta > 0 AND m.s2_delta > 0 AND m.s3_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower in ALL sectors'
        WHEN m.s1_delta > 0 AND m.s2_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower in S1+S2 (T4/T11 area)'
        WHEN m.s1_delta > 0 AND m.s3_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower in S1+S3 (T4/T14 area)'
        WHEN m.s2_delta > 0 AND m.s3_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower in S2+S3 (T11/T14 area)'
        WHEN m.s1_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower only in S1 (T4 area)'
        WHEN m.s2_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower only in S2 (T11 area)'
        WHEN m.s3_delta > 0 THEN 'Norris slower only in S3 (T14 area)'
        ELSE 'Norris faster in all sectors'
    END AS sector_loss_pattern,
    m.norris_pos,
    m.russell_pos,
    m.track_flag,
    a.laps_compared,
    a.avg_lap_delta,
    a.avg_s1_delta,
    a.avg_s2_delta,
    a.avg_s3_delta,
    a.stddev_s1_delta,
    a.stddev_s2_delta,
    a.stddev_s3_delta,
    a.laps_norris_slower_all_sectors,
    a.laps_norris_slower_s1,
    a.laps_norris_slower_s2,
    a.laps_norris_slower_s3
FROM matched m
CROSS JOIN aggregated a
ORDER BY m.lap_number
LIMIT 200

results.question: Compare mini-sector dominance maps for Verstappen vs Piastri across Q1, Q2, and Q3 at Qatar 2025 — did Verstappen's high-speed advantage strengthen as fuel dropped?
results.author_note: RacingNews365 / Qatar Q-segment evolution pieces tracking pace shift across Q1/Q2/Q3.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: The data covers a single combined qualifying session (session_key 9850) rather than separate Q1/Q2/Q3 splits, so per-segment comparison is not possible. Across the overall sector dominance map, Piastri was faster in all three sectors: he led S1, S2, and S3 with Verstappen trailing in each. On a lap-by-lap basis, Piastri was quicker on laps 1, 11, 12, 13, 14, and 15 (gaps ranging from ~0.25s to ~1.0s in his favour), while Verstappen was faster on lap 10 by ~1.14s. There is no evidence in these rows that Verstappen's high-speed advantage strengthened as the session progressed — Piastri dominated the majority of timed laps and all three sector bests.
results.answerReasoning: The SECTOR_DOMINANCE_MAP rows show Piastri with sector_rank=1 in S1, S2, and S3, meaning he set the best time in every sector overall. The LAP_BY_LAP_DELTA rows show Piastri faster on laps 1, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and Verstappen faster only on lap 10. The query does not separate Q1/Q2/Q3 (no session_name filter or sub-session breakdown), so the fuel-load progression question cannot be answered with the available data. No trend of Verstappen closing or extending a gap across qualifying phases is visible.
results.generationNotes: Since session_key 9850 covers the Qatar 2025 qualifying session (Q1/Q2/Q3 are sub-segments within the same session key), I build a two-part analysis: (1) a sector dominance map showing which driver (Verstappen #1 vs Piastri #81) posted the best time in each of S1, S2, S3 across all valid laps, with gap-to-leader per sector — S1 at Qatar is the high-speed blast through the back straight complex, S2 covers the technical middle sector, and S3 the final chicane sequence; (2) a lap-by-lap delta table showing each driver's lap time and per-sector deviation from the two-driver average on that lap number, which lets us trace whether Verstappen's S1 advantage widened on later (lower fuel/tyre) laps. The UNION ALL combines both views into one result set. Note: OpenF1 does not split Q1/Q2/Q3 into separate session_keys for the same event, so lap_number progression serves as the proxy for qualifying phase advancement. The 'fuel drop' framing is interpreted as tyre/lap progression within the session. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9850)
results.sql:
WITH q_laps AS (
    SELECT
        le.session_key,
        le.session_name,
        le.driver_number,
        le.driver_name,
        le.team_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy,
        le.compound_name,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap,
        le.position_end_of_lap,
        le.track_flag
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    WHERE le.session_key = 9850
      AND le.driver_number IN (1, 81)
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_1 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_2 IS NOT NULL
      AND le.duration_sector_3 IS NOT NULL
),
best_laps AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        MIN(lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE is_valid) AS best_valid_lap,
        MIN(duration_sector_1) FILTER (WHERE is_valid) AS best_s1,
        MIN(duration_sector_2) FILTER (WHERE is_valid) AS best_s2,
        MIN(duration_sector_3) FILTER (WHERE is_valid) AS best_s3,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_valid) AS valid_lap_count,
        COUNT(*) AS total_laps
    FROM q_laps
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name
),
sector_dominance AS (
    SELECT
        'S1' AS sector,
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        best_s1 AS best_sector_time
    FROM best_laps
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        'S2' AS sector,
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        best_s2 AS best_sector_time
    FROM best_laps
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        'S3' AS sector,
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        best_s3 AS best_sector_time
    FROM best_laps
),
sector_winner AS (
    SELECT
        sector,
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        best_sector_time,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY sector ORDER BY best_sector_time ASC) AS sector_rank,
        best_sector_time - MIN(best_sector_time) OVER (PARTITION BY sector) AS gap_to_best_in_sector
    FROM sector_dominance
    WHERE best_sector_time IS NOT NULL
),
lap_by_lap AS (
    SELECT
        q.lap_number,
        q.driver_number,
        q.driver_name,
        q.lap_duration,
        q.duration_sector_1,
        q.duration_sector_2,
        q.duration_sector_3,
        q.is_valid,
        q.is_personal_best_proxy,
        q.tyre_age_on_lap,
        -- S1 delta between drivers on same lap number (approximation)
        q.duration_sector_1 - AVG(q.duration_sector_1) OVER (PARTITION BY q.lap_number) AS s1_vs_avg,
        q.duration_sector_2 - AVG(q.duration_sector_2) OVER (PARTITION BY q.lap_number) AS s2_vs_avg,
        q.duration_sector_3 - AVG(q.duration_sector_3) OVER (PARTITION BY q.lap_number) AS s3_vs_avg,
        q.lap_duration - AVG(q.lap_duration) OVER (PARTITION BY q.lap_number) AS lap_vs_avg
    FROM q_laps q
)
SELECT
    'SECTOR_DOMINANCE_MAP' AS analysis_type,
    sw.sector,
    sw.driver_number,
    sw.driver_name,
    ROUND(sw.best_sector_time::numeric, 3) AS best_sector_time,
    sw.sector_rank,
    ROUND(sw.gap_to_best_in_sector::numeric, 3) AS gap_to_sector_leader,
    CASE WHEN sw.sector_rank = 1 THEN 'DOMINANT' ELSE 'TRAILING' END AS dominance_status,
    bl.best_valid_lap,
    bl.valid_lap_count,
    bl.total_laps
FROM sector_winner sw
JOIN best_laps bl ON sw.driver_number = bl.driver_number
UNION ALL
SELECT
    'LAP_BY_LAP_DELTA' AS analysis_type,
    'LAP_' || lbl.lap_number::text AS sector,
    lbl.driver_number,
    lbl.driver_name,
    ROUND(lbl.lap_duration::numeric, 3) AS best_sector_time,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY lbl.lap_number ORDER BY lbl.lap_duration ASC) AS sector_rank,
    ROUND(lbl.lap_vs_avg::numeric, 3) AS gap_to_sector_leader,
    CASE
        WHEN lbl.is_personal_best_proxy THEN 'PERSONAL_BEST'
        WHEN lbl.is_valid THEN 'VALID'
        ELSE 'INVALID'
    END AS dominance_status,
    ROUND(lbl.s1_vs_avg::numeric, 3) AS best_valid_lap,
    ROUND(lbl.s2_vs_avg::numeric, 3) AS valid_lap_count,
    ROUND(lbl.s3_vs_avg::numeric, 3) AS total_laps
FROM lap_by_lap lbl
ORDER BY analysis_type, sector, sector_rank
LIMIT 200

results.question: Compare Hamilton and Leclerc on the traffic-handling axis at Monaco 2025.
results.author_note: Sky Sports F1 Ted Kravitz Notebook + Autosport Monaco 2025 review on Ferrari's blue-flag / backmarker management.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Hamilton encountered traffic far more frequently than Leclerc at Monaco 2025, with 74 likely traffic laps (50.0% of clean, flag-free laps) versus Leclerc's 52 (35.1%). However, Leclerc was more severely impacted when caught in traffic: his average delta to the fastest lap on traffic laps was 5.714s compared to Hamilton's 5.407s, and his incremental traffic penalty (traffic delta minus free-air delta) was 3.028s versus Hamilton's 2.103s. Hamilton's free-air average delta was also higher (3.304s vs 2.686s), suggesting Leclerc was generally quicker in clean air. In summary, Hamilton spent more laps in traffic but managed it better relative to his own baseline, while Leclerc was faster in free air but lost more time per traffic lap.
results.answerReasoning: The rows directly provide traffic_lap_pct (50.00 vs 35.14), avg_delta_on_traffic_laps (5.407 vs 5.714), avg_delta_on_free_laps (3.304 vs 2.686), and incremental_traffic_delta (2.103 vs 3.028) for Hamilton and Leclerc respectively. These metrics together characterise both the frequency and severity of traffic impact for each driver.
results.generationNotes: Traffic-handling at Monaco is best proxied by identifying laps where a driver was clearly slower than their personal best pace despite clean conditions (no yellow/SC/VSC/red flags, no pit in/out laps, valid laps). I define a 'likely_traffic_lap' as a valid, non-pit, non-flag-affected lap where delta_to_fastest > 2.0 seconds — a meaningful threshold at Monaco where the circuit is narrow and overtaking is nearly impossible, so pace loss in clean conditions strongly implies traffic. The query computes: (1) how many such laps each driver had, (2) what percentage of their clean-condition laps were traffic-affected, (3) the average time cost on those laps vs. their personal best and vs. their free-air laps, and (4) the incremental delta (traffic laps minus free-air laps) as a measure of how much traffic hurt each driver. This gives a direct comparison of Hamilton vs. Leclerc on the traffic-handling axis for Monaco 2025 (session_key=9979). | The error 'function round(double precision, integer) does not exist' occurs because PostgreSQL's ROUND(x, n) with a scale argument only accepts numeric, not double precision. The fix is to cast all double precision values to ::numeric before passing them to ROUND(). This applies to all ROUND() calls in both the traffic_summary CTE and the final SELECT, including computed expressions like avg_traffic_lap - personal_best_lap and avg_delta_on_traffic_laps - avg_delta_on_free_laps. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_verified(session_key=9979)
results.sql:
WITH lap_data AS (
    SELECT
        le.driver_number,
        le.driver_name,
        le.team_name,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.duration_sector_1,
        le.duration_sector_2,
        le.duration_sector_3,
        le.is_valid,
        le.is_pit_out_lap,
        le.is_pit_lap,
        le.position_end_of_lap,
        le.track_flag,
        le.compound_name,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap,
        le.stint_number,
        le.delta_to_fastest,
        le.pct_from_fastest,
        le.delta_to_rep,
        le.pct_from_rep,
        le.is_personal_best_proxy,
        le.fastest_valid_lap,
        le.lap_rep_time,
        CASE
            WHEN le.track_flag IN ('YELLOW','SC','VSC','RED') THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_flag_affected,
        CASE
            WHEN le.is_valid = TRUE
              AND le.is_pit_out_lap = FALSE
              AND le.is_pit_lap = FALSE
              AND le.track_flag NOT IN ('YELLOW','SC','VSC','RED')
              AND le.delta_to_fastest IS NOT NULL
              AND le.delta_to_fastest > 2.0
            THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS likely_traffic_lap
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    WHERE le.session_key = 9979
      AND le.driver_number IN (16, 44)
),
traffic_summary AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        COUNT(*) AS total_laps,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_valid = TRUE AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE AND is_pit_lap = FALSE) AS clean_laps,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE likely_traffic_lap = TRUE) AS likely_traffic_laps,
        ROUND((100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE likely_traffic_lap = TRUE)
            / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_valid = TRUE AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE AND is_pit_lap = FALSE AND is_flag_affected = FALSE), 0))::numeric, 2) AS traffic_lap_pct,
        MIN(lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE is_valid = TRUE AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE AND is_pit_lap = FALSE) AS personal_best_lap,
        ROUND(AVG(lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE is_valid = TRUE AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE AND is_pit_lap = FALSE AND is_flag_affected = FALSE)::numeric, 3) AS avg_clean_lap,
        ROUND(AVG(lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE likely_traffic_lap = TRUE)::numeric, 3) AS avg_traffic_lap,
        ROUND(AVG(delta_to_fastest) FILTER (WHERE likely_traffic_lap = TRUE)::numeric, 3) AS avg_delta_on_traffic_laps,
        ROUND(AVG(delta_to_fastest) FILTER (WHERE is_valid = TRUE AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE AND is_pit_lap = FALSE AND is_flag_affected = FALSE AND likely_traffic_lap = FALSE)::numeric, 3) AS avg_delta_on_free_laps,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_flag_affected = TRUE) AS flag_affected_laps,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_pit_out_lap = TRUE) AS pit_out_laps,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_pit_lap = TRUE) AS pit_in_laps
    FROM lap_data
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name
)
SELECT
    ts.driver_number,
    ts.driver_name,
    ts.team_name,
    ts.total_laps,
    ts.clean_laps,
    ts.likely_traffic_laps,
    ts.traffic_lap_pct,
    ts.personal_best_lap,
    ts.avg_clean_lap,
    ts.avg_traffic_lap,
    ROUND((ts.avg_traffic_lap - ts.personal_best_lap)::numeric, 3) AS avg_traffic_cost_vs_best,
    ts.avg_delta_on_traffic_laps,
    ts.avg_delta_on_free_laps,
    ROUND((ts.avg_delta_on_traffic_laps - ts.avg_delta_on_free_laps)::numeric, 3) AS incremental_traffic_delta,
    ts.flag_affected_laps,
    ts.pit_out_laps,
    ts.pit_in_laps
FROM traffic_summary ts
ORDER BY ts.driver_number
LIMIT 10

results.question: For each driver who scored points at both Spa and Monza 2025, compare their overtake-difficulty axis at Spa vs their tyre-management axis at Monza, and flag any driver whose stronger axis flipped between the two weekends.
results.author_note: Mark Hughes Motorsport.com Monza technical column contrasting low-deg/high-overtaking tracks vs tyre-limited circuits.
results.adequacyReason: The query returned no rows, so the question remains unanswered.
results.answer: No rows matched this question with the current context. Caveats: raw.overtakes is currently empty in this warehouse.
results.generationNotes: The question asks to compare two distinct performance axes across two 2025 race weekends (Spa and Monza) for drivers who scored points at both. Since raw.overtakes is empty per the completeness warning, I proxy the 'overtake-difficulty axis' at Spa using positions_gained from core.grid_vs_finish — a driver who gained many positions overcame overtake difficulty. For the 'tyre-management axis' at Monza I use average degradation_per_lap from core.stint_summary (inverted so higher = better tyre management). I identify both race sessions by circuit/location name matching, filter to points-scorers via raw.session_result, join the two axes, compute each driver's deviation from the field average on each axis, and flag any driver whose relative strength flipped (above-average on one axis but below-average on the other). The runtime context pins session_key=9912 but the question is cross-event, so I resolve both Spa and Monza 2025 race sessions dynamically. | The original query was structurally correct but the database error 'Only a single SQL statement is allowed' suggests there may have been a trailing semicolon or extra statement appended. The fix is to ensure the entire query is one clean CTE-based SELECT with no trailing semicolons or extra statements. The logic is preserved: find 2025 Race sessions at Spa (Belgium) and Monza (Italy), identify drivers who scored points at both, compute a spa overtake-difficulty axis (positions_gained from grid_vs_finish) and a monza tyre-management axis (negative avg degradation_per_lap from stint_summary), normalize each against the field average using window functions, then flag drivers whose dominant axis flipped between the two weekends. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_unverifiable_no_literal_session_key_predicate(session_key=9912)

results.sql:
WITH spa_sessions AS (
    SELECT s.session_key, s.meeting_key
    FROM core.sessions s
    WHERE s.year = 2025
      AND s.session_type = 'Race'
      AND (s.circuit_short_name ILIKE '%spa%' OR s.location ILIKE '%spa%' OR s.country_name ILIKE '%belgium%')
),
monza_sessions AS (
    SELECT s.session_key, s.meeting_key
    FROM core.sessions s
    WHERE s.year = 2025
      AND s.session_type = 'Race'
      AND (s.circuit_short_name ILIKE '%monza%' OR s.location ILIKE '%monza%' OR s.country_name ILIKE '%italy%')
),
spa_points AS (
    SELECT sr.driver_number, sr.points AS spa_points, sr.position AS spa_finish
    FROM raw.session_result sr
    JOIN spa_sessions ss ON sr.session_key = ss.session_key
    WHERE sr.points > 0
),
monza_points AS (
    SELECT sr.driver_number, sr.points AS monza_points, sr.position AS monza_finish
    FROM raw.session_result sr
    JOIN monza_sessions ms ON sr.session_key = ms.session_key
    WHERE sr.points > 0
),
both_events AS (
    SELECT sp.driver_number, sp.spa_points, sp.spa_finish, mp.monza_points, mp.monza_finish
    FROM spa_points sp
    JOIN monza_points mp ON sp.driver_number = mp.driver_number
),
spa_overtake AS (
    SELECT gvf.driver_number,
           gvf.positions_gained AS spa_positions_gained,
           COALESCE(gvf.positions_gained, 0) AS spa_overtake_axis
    FROM core.grid_vs_finish gvf
    JOIN spa_sessions ss ON gvf.session_key = ss.session_key
),
monza_tyre AS (
    SELECT st.driver_number,
           AVG(st.degradation_per_lap) AS monza_avg_degradation,
           COUNT(st.stint_number) AS monza_stints,
           -1.0 * COALESCE(AVG(st.degradation_per_lap), 0) AS monza_tyre_axis
    FROM core.stint_summary st
    JOIN monza_sessions ms ON st.session_key = ms.session_key
    WHERE st.session_type = 'Race'
    GROUP BY st.driver_number
),
combined AS (
    SELECT
        be.driver_number,
        COALESCE(dd.full_name, CAST(be.driver_number AS TEXT)) AS driver_name,
        be.spa_points,
        be.spa_finish,
        be.monza_points,
        be.monza_finish,
        so.spa_overtake_axis,
        so.spa_positions_gained,
        mt.monza_tyre_axis,
        mt.monza_avg_degradation,
        mt.monza_stints,
        so.spa_overtake_axis - AVG(so.spa_overtake_axis) OVER () AS spa_overtake_vs_avg,
        mt.monza_tyre_axis - AVG(mt.monza_tyre_axis) OVER () AS monza_tyre_vs_avg
    FROM both_events be
    LEFT JOIN spa_overtake so ON be.driver_number = so.driver_number
    LEFT JOIN monza_tyre mt ON be.driver_number = mt.driver_number
    LEFT JOIN core.driver_dim dd ON be.driver_number::TEXT = dd.driver_number::TEXT
)
SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    spa_points,
    spa_finish,
    monza_points,
    monza_finish,
    ROUND(spa_overtake_axis::NUMERIC, 3)       AS spa_overtake_axis,
    ROUND(spa_overtake_vs_avg::NUMERIC, 3)     AS spa_overtake_vs_field_avg,
    ROUND(monza_tyre_axis::NUMERIC, 4)         AS monza_tyre_axis,
    ROUND(monza_tyre_vs_avg::NUMERIC, 4)       AS monza_tyre_vs_field_avg,
    monza_avg_degradation                      AS monza_avg_deg_per_lap,
    monza_stints,
    CASE WHEN spa_overtake_vs_avg > 0 THEN 'overtake_strength' ELSE 'overtake_weakness' END AS spa_dominant_axis,
    CASE WHEN monza_tyre_vs_avg > 0 THEN 'tyre_mgmt_strength' ELSE 'tyre_mgmt_weakness' END AS monza_dominant_axis,
    CASE
        WHEN spa_overtake_vs_avg > 0 AND monza_tyre_vs_avg <= 0 THEN TRUE
        WHEN spa_overtake_vs_avg <= 0 AND monza_tyre_vs_avg > 0 THEN TRUE
        ELSE FALSE
    END AS axis_flipped_between_weekends
FROM combined
ORDER BY axis_flipped_between_weekends DESC, spa_points DESC
LIMIT 30

results.question: Across the 2025 season, which driver has the largest delta between their restart-axis score and their race-pace-axis score, and is that delta widening or narrowing in the second half of the year?
results.author_note: Scott Mitchell-Malm 'stat that matters' + RaceFans season-trend driver rankings.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Yuki Tsunoda has the largest delta between his restart-axis score and race-pace-axis score in 2025, with an overall average absolute delta of approximately 1.20 across 27 race entries and a maximum single-race delta of 5.61. The delta is slightly widening in the second half of the season: his average absolute axis delta rises from 1.12 in the first half (12 races with Red Bull Racing) to 1.26 in the second half (15 races), suggesting the gap between his restart performance and raw race pace is modestly increasing rather than narrowing.
results.answerReasoning: The query identifies driver 22 (Yuki Tsunoda) as the top driver by average absolute axis delta. The rows show two team contexts (Racing Bulls and Red Bull Racing) due to his mid-season team change. Using the larger 27-race dataset, first-half avg_abs_axis_delta is 1.1164 and second-half is 1.2648, indicating a slight widening. The overall_max_abs_delta of 5.6066 confirms at least one race with a very large divergence between his restart and pace scores.
results.generationNotes: The question asks for the driver with the largest gap between a 'restart-axis score' and a 'race-pace-axis score' across 2025, and whether that gap is widening or narrowing in the second half. Since there is no explicit 'restart score' column in the schema, I proxy it with positions_gained from core.grid_vs_finish (positions gained from grid to finish captures how well a driver capitalises on race starts/restarts). Race pace is proxied by median valid lap time from core.laps_enriched. Both metrics are z-scored per race so they are on a comparable scale (pace z-score is negated so higher = better for both axes). The axis delta is (z_restart - z_pace); a large absolute delta means the driver is much stronger on one axis than the other. I find the driver with the highest average absolute delta across all 2025 races, then split their races into first-half and second-half of the season to assess whether the delta is widening or narrowing. The runtime context pins session_key=9839 and driver_number=1, but the question is a season-wide aggregate, so I use year=2025 and session_type='Race' as the primary filter and let the top-driver CTE surface whoever has the largest delta (which may or may not be driver 1). | session_pin_unverifiable_no_literal_session_key_predicate(session_key=9839)
results.sql:
WITH race_sessions_2025 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT s.session_key, s.meeting_key, s.date_start, s.country_name, s.location
    FROM core.sessions s
    WHERE s.year = 2025
      AND s.session_type = 'Race'
),
driver_race_pace AS (
    -- Race pace axis: median valid lap time per driver per race (lower = better)
    SELECT
        le.session_key,
        le.driver_number,
        le.driver_name,
        le.team_name,
        PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY le.lap_duration) AS median_valid_lap
    FROM core.laps_enriched le
    INNER JOIN race_sessions_2025 rs ON le.session_key = rs.session_key
    WHERE le.is_valid = TRUE
      AND le.is_pit_out_lap = FALSE
      AND le.is_pit_lap = FALSE
      AND le.lap_duration IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY le.session_key, le.driver_number, le.driver_name, le.team_name
),
restart_proxy AS (
    -- Restart axis proxy: positions gained from grid to finish (higher = better restarter)
    SELECT
        gf.session_key,
        gf.driver_number,
        gf.driver_name,
        gf.team_name,
        gf.positions_gained
    FROM core.grid_vs_finish gf
    INNER JOIN race_sessions_2025 rs ON gf.session_key = rs.session_key
    WHERE gf.grid_position IS NOT NULL
      AND gf.finish_position IS NOT NULL
),
per_race AS (
    SELECT
        rp.session_key,
        rs.date_start,
        rs.country_name,
        rp.driver_number,
        rp.driver_name,
        rp.team_name,
        rp.positions_gained AS restart_score,
        drp.median_valid_lap AS pace_score
    FROM restart_proxy rp
    INNER JOIN race_sessions_2025 rs ON rp.session_key = rs.session_key
    LEFT JOIN driver_race_pace drp
        ON rp.session_key = drp.session_key
        AND rp.driver_number = drp.driver_number
    WHERE drp.median_valid_lap IS NOT NULL
),
normalized AS (
    -- Normalize both axes per race to z-scores so they are comparable
    SELECT
        pr.*,
        AVG(pr.restart_score) OVER (PARTITION BY pr.session_key) AS avg_restart,
        STDDEV(pr.restart_score) OVER (PARTITION BY pr.session_key) AS std_restart,
        AVG(pr.pace_score) OVER (PARTITION BY pr.session_key) AS avg_pace,
        STDDEV(pr.pace_score) OVER (PARTITION BY pr.session_key) AS std_pace
    FROM per_race pr
),
z_scored AS (
    SELECT
        n.session_key,
        n.date_start,
        n.country_name,
        n.driver_number,
        n.driver_name,
        n.team_name,
        CASE WHEN n.std_restart > 0 THEN (n.restart_score - n.avg_restart) / n.std_restart ELSE 0 END AS z_restart,
        -- Invert pace z-score: lower lap time = better, so negate
        CASE WHEN n.std_pace > 0 THEN -1.0 * (n.pace_score - n.avg_pace) / n.std_pace ELSE 0 END AS z_pace
    FROM normalized n
),
driver_axis_delta AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        session_key,
        date_start,
        country_name,
        z_restart,
        z_pace,
        (z_restart - z_pace) AS axis_delta,
        ABS(z_restart - z_pace) AS abs_axis_delta,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number ORDER BY date_start) AS race_seq,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_number) AS total_races
    FROM z_scored
),
driver_overall AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        AVG(ABS(axis_delta)) AS avg_abs_delta,
        MAX(ABS(axis_delta)) AS max_abs_delta,
        COUNT(*) AS races_counted
    FROM driver_axis_delta
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name
),
top_driver AS (
    SELECT driver_number
    FROM driver_overall
    ORDER BY avg_abs_delta DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
half_split AS (
    SELECT
        dad.driver_number,
        dad.driver_name,
        dad.team_name,
        dad.session_key,
        dad.date_start,
        dad.country_name,
        dad.z_restart,
        dad.z_pace,
        dad.axis_delta,
        dad.abs_axis_delta,
        dad.race_seq,
        dad.total_races,
        CASE WHEN dad.race_seq <= dad.total_races / 2 THEN 'first_half' ELSE 'second_half' END AS season_half
    FROM driver_axis_delta dad
    INNER JOIN top_driver td ON dad.driver_number = td.driver_number
),
half_summary AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        season_half,
        AVG(abs_axis_delta) AS avg_abs_delta_in_half,
        AVG(axis_delta) AS avg_signed_delta_in_half,
        COUNT(*) AS races_in_half
    FROM half_split
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name, season_half
),
final AS (
    SELECT
        hs.driver_number,
        hs.driver_name,
        hs.team_name,
        hs.season_half,
        hs.avg_abs_delta_in_half,
        hs.avg_signed_delta_in_half,
        hs.races_in_half,
        do2.avg_abs_delta AS overall_avg_abs_delta,
        do2.max_abs_delta AS overall_max_abs_delta,
        do2.races_counted AS total_races_2025
    FROM half_summary hs
    INNER JOIN driver_overall do2 ON hs.driver_number = do2.driver_number
)
SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    team_name,
    season_half,
    ROUND(avg_abs_delta_in_half::numeric, 4) AS avg_abs_axis_delta,
    ROUND(avg_signed_delta_in_half::numeric, 4) AS avg_signed_axis_delta,
    races_in_half,
    ROUND(overall_avg_abs_delta::numeric, 4) AS overall_avg_abs_delta,
    ROUND(overall_max_abs_delta::numeric, 4) AS overall_max_abs_delta,
    total_races_2025
FROM final
ORDER BY driver_number, season_half
LIMIT 10

results.question: How many penalty points were issued by stewards at the 2025 São Paulo Grand Prix?

results.question: Did the lap-6 Piastri-Antonelli-Leclerc Turn 1 contact at São Paulo 2025 swing more points away from Piastri or away from Leclerc, after their finishing positions and any time penalties are applied?
results.author_note: Motorsport.com 'Why Piastri was penalised for F1 Brazil GP clash' — wholly responsible verdict.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Based on the available data, the incident hurt Leclerc more than Piastri in terms of points. Piastri started 3rd and finished 2nd (gaining 1 position, scoring 18 points), while Leclerc started 5th and finished 4th (gaining 1 position, scoring 12 points). Antonelli, the third driver involved, started 14th and finished 15th (losing 1 position, scoring 0 points). However, the data contains no record of any time penalties applied to any of the three drivers, and no lap-6 position data is available to confirm the immediate impact of the contact. Without knowing what positions Piastri and Leclerc held before and after the Turn 1 incident, or any stewards' penalty details, it is not possible to definitively attribute a points swing to the contact itself — only that Leclerc ended up with 6 fewer points than Piastri (12 vs. 18).
results.answerReasoning: The rows show final finishing positions and points scored for all three drivers. Piastri scored 18 pts (P2), Leclerc scored 12 pts (P4), and Antonelli scored 0 pts (P15). No time penalties appear in the data, and position_end_of_lap is null for laps 5–7 for all drivers, making it impossible to trace the direct positional impact of the lap-6 contact. The 6-point gap between Piastri and Leclerc is the closest proxy available, but cannot be solely attributed to the incident without pre/post-contact position data or penalty records.
results.generationNotes: The question asks about the championship-points impact of the lap-6 Turn 1 contact between Piastri (81), Antonelli (12), and Leclerc (16) at São Paulo 2025 (session_key 9839). I pull grid/finish positions, positions gained, and actual championship points scored (using a standard F1 points-map CTE) for all three drivers, plus their session-level pace summary. I also pull laps 5–8 detail (lap duration, position at end of lap, track flags, validity) to surface any lap-time or position disruption around the incident. This gives the analyst everything needed to reason about whether Piastri or Leclerc lost more points relative to their expected finishing positions before the contact. Driver numbers: Piastri=81, Antonelli=12, Leclerc=16. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9839)
results.sql:
WITH drivers_of_interest AS (
  SELECT
    sd.driver_number,
    sd.full_name,
    sd.team_name
  FROM core.session_drivers sd
  WHERE sd.session_key = 9839
    AND sd.driver_number IN (81, 12, 16)
),
grid_finish AS (
  SELECT
    gf.driver_number,
    gf.driver_name,
    gf.team_name,
    gf.grid_position,
    gf.finish_position,
    gf.positions_gained
  FROM core.grid_vs_finish gf
  WHERE gf.session_key = 9839
    AND gf.driver_number IN (81, 12, 16)
),
points_map (finish_position, championship_points) AS (
  VALUES
    (1, 25), (2, 18), (3, 15), (4, 12), (5, 10),
    (6, 8),  (7, 6),  (8, 4),  (9, 2),  (10, 1)
),
session_summary AS (
  SELECT
    dss.driver_number,
    dss.driver_name,
    dss.team_name,
    dss.grid_position,
    dss.finish_position,
    dss.positions_gained,
    dss.best_valid_lap,
    dss.avg_valid_lap,
    dss.pit_stop_count,
    dss.strategy_type,
    dss.compounds_used
  FROM core.driver_session_summary dss
  WHERE dss.session_key = 9839
    AND dss.driver_number IN (81, 12, 16)
),
lap6_context AS (
  SELECT
    le.driver_number,
    le.driver_name,
    le.lap_number,
    le.lap_duration,
    le.position_end_of_lap,
    le.track_flag,
    le.is_valid,
    le.invalid_reason,
    le.delta_to_fastest
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9839
    AND le.driver_number IN (81, 12, 16)
    AND le.lap_number BETWEEN 5 AND 8
  ORDER BY le.driver_number, le.lap_number
)
SELECT
  ss.driver_number,
  ss.driver_name,
  ss.team_name,
  ss.grid_position,
  ss.finish_position,
  ss.positions_gained,
  COALESCE(pm.championship_points, 0) AS points_scored,
  ss.best_valid_lap,
  ss.avg_valid_lap,
  ss.pit_stop_count,
  ss.strategy_type,
  ss.compounds_used,
  (
    SELECT json_agg(
      json_build_object(
        'lap', lc.lap_number,
        'lap_duration', lc.lap_duration,
        'position_end_of_lap', lc.position_end_of_lap,
        'track_flag', lc.track_flag,
        'is_valid', lc.is_valid,
        'invalid_reason', lc.invalid_reason,
        'delta_to_fastest', lc.delta_to_fastest
      ) ORDER BY lc.lap_number
    )
    FROM lap6_context lc
    WHERE lc.driver_number = ss.driver_number
  ) AS laps_5_to_8_detail
FROM session_summary ss
LEFT JOIN points_map pm ON pm.finish_position = ss.finish_position
ORDER BY
  CASE ss.driver_number WHEN 81 THEN 1 WHEN 16 THEN 2 WHEN 12 THEN 3 END

results.question: Across the 2025 São Paulo GP weekend (sprint + race), aggregate every steward decision affecting top-3 championship runners and compute net championship-points delta for Norris, Piastri and Verstappen.
results.author_note: F1.com + GRANDPRIX247 + PlanetF1: Verstappen sprint 5s, Piastri race 10s + 2pts, Norris formation-lap punishment, Mercedes tyre-pressure fine.
results.adequacyReason: The query returned no rows, so the question remains unanswered.
results.answer: No rows matched this question with the current context.
results.generationNotes: The query targets the 2025 São Paulo GP weekend (meeting_key=1275) across Sprint and Race session types. It: (1) identifies the relevant sessions from core.sessions filtered by meeting_key and session_type; (2) pulls raw.race_control entries for drivers 4 (Norris), 81 (Piastri), and 1 (Verstappen) where the message or category indicates a steward action (penalty, reprimand, warning, disqualification, etc.); (3) retrieves official points from raw.session_result for those drivers across both sessions — official results already incorporate any time penalties applied; (4) aggregates steward decisions per driver with detail strings; (5) joins points and steward data to present total weekend points (net championship delta) alongside a count and description of all steward decisions. The runtime context confirms meeting_key=1275 and driver_number=1 (Verstappen) is in scope, and the query extends to all three championship contenders as requested. | session_pin_unverifiable_no_literal_session_key_predicate(session_key=9845)
results.sql:
WITH target_sessions AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT s.session_key, s.session_name, s.session_type
    FROM core.sessions s
    WHERE s.meeting_key = 1275
      AND s.session_type IN ('Race', 'Sprint')
),
target_drivers AS (
    SELECT driver_number, full_name
    FROM (
        VALUES
            (4,  'Lando Norris'),
            (81, 'Oscar Piastri'),
            (1,  'Max Verstappen')
    ) AS t(driver_number, full_name)
),
steward_decisions AS (
    SELECT
        rc.session_key,
        ts.session_name,
        ts.session_type,
        rc.date,
        rc.driver_number,
        rc.message,
        rc.category,
        rc.flag,
        rc.lap_number
    FROM raw.race_control rc
    JOIN target_sessions ts ON ts.session_key = rc.session_key
    WHERE rc.driver_number::int IN (4, 81, 1)
      AND (
          rc.category ILIKE '%steward%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%penalty%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%time penalty%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%drive through%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%stop and go%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%reprimand%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%warning%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%disqualif%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%grid%penalty%'
          OR rc.message ILIKE '%points%'
      )
),
points_earned AS (
    SELECT
        sr.session_key,
        ts.session_name,
        ts.session_type,
        sr.driver_number,
        td.full_name,
        sr.position,
        sr.points,
        sr.status,
        sr.classified
    FROM raw.session_result sr
    JOIN target_sessions ts ON ts.session_key = sr.session_key
    JOIN target_drivers td ON td.driver_number = sr.driver_number::int
    WHERE sr.driver_number::int IN (4, 81, 1)
),
points_agg AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        full_name,
        SUM(points) AS total_points_weekend,
        STRING_AGG(session_type || ': P' || position::text || ' (' || points::text || ' pts)', ' | ' ORDER BY session_key) AS session_breakdown
    FROM points_earned
    GROUP BY driver_number, full_name
),
steward_agg AS (
    SELECT
        sd.driver_number::int AS driver_number,
        COUNT(*) AS total_steward_decisions,
        STRING_AGG(
            sd.session_type || ' Lap ' || COALESCE(sd.lap_number::text, '?') || ': ' || sd.message,
            ' || ' ORDER BY sd.date
        ) AS decisions_detail
    FROM steward_decisions sd
    GROUP BY sd.driver_number::int
)
SELECT
    pa.driver_number,
    pa.full_name,
    pa.total_points_weekend,
    pa.session_breakdown,
    COALESCE(sa.total_steward_decisions, 0) AS steward_decisions_count,
    COALESCE(sa.decisions_detail, 'No steward decisions found') AS steward_decisions_detail,
    -- Net delta is points actually scored (penalties already reflected in official results)
    pa.total_points_weekend AS net_championship_points_delta
FROM points_agg pa
LEFT JOIN steward_agg sa ON sa.driver_number = pa.driver_number
ORDER BY pa.total_points_weekend DESC
LIMIT 10
